name: Qing Dynasty (Manchu)
note: Manchu rulers governed Han majority but maintained strict Manchu identity: forbidden intermarriage, maintained bannerman system, Manchu language in court. Vajrayana Buddhism was a deliberate tool to legitimize rule over Mongols and Tibetans. Projected Confucian face outward while preserving Manchu identity internally — a masterclass in dual identity governance

figures.name: Nurhaci
figures.significance: United the Jurchen tribes; created the Eight Banner military system

figures.name: Kangxi Emperor
figures.significance: Longest-reigning Chinese emperor (61 years); consolidated Qing rule

figures.name: Qianlong Emperor
figures.significance: Greatest territorial extent; commissioned the Siku Quanshu — the largest library in history

figures.name: Puyi
figures.significance: Last Emperor of China; the final chapter of 2000 years of imperial rule
